domain: bbchallenge certificate techniques for TM nonhalting: the CTL family (NGramCPS, RepWL, FAR, WFAR), Bouncers, the loop deciders, mxdys's 2026 FAR variant, and the empirical certificate-expressivity hierarchy from the BB5 determination — read from Bouncers.v (busycoq), the BB5 paper Sections 4-7, the bbchallenge-deciders FAR/Bouncers source, and mxdys's ccz181078/TM FAR-branch C++ (3113 lines)
weird_unnoticed: mxdys's 2026 holdout-killer (the "new FAR variant" that solved 113/1534 BB6 holdouts) is not a new decider at all — it is a UCB multi-armed bandit over a zoo of hand-crafted CACHE-EVICTION POLICIES used as abstract domains. In ccz181078/TM (branch FAR) main.cpp, every "DFA generator" (CPS_LRU, CPS_LRU_BD, LRUpair, RWL_mod, RWS_mod, RNGS_mod, unique_wall, ExRWL, RndDFA) is a finite-memory fold over the half-tape's block stream whose state-update rule is literally a cache policy: LRU eviction, FIFO queue, bounded sorted set, move-to-front, or random replacement (RndDFA lazily builds a RANDOM DFA transition on first query). Each fold of finite range induces a finite-index right congruence on half-tape words; the decider lazily constructs the reachable Myhill-Nerode quotient and runs CTL closure or BEM97-style pre* saturation on it. Compute across the (policy, hyperparameter) arms is allocated by a UCB bandit (ucb.txt checkpoints per-arm statistics; reward = machines solved per microsecond; the exploration constant is randomly toggled between 1.0 and 0.01 on every update — `double c=mt()%2?1:0.01`, a bi-level exploration hack I have never seen in the bandit literature). The pipeline's winning hyperparameters then get frozen verbatim into the formal Coq proof (Coq-BB5's Decider_RepWL_Hardcoded_Parameters.v hardcodes 6,577 grid-searched (l,T) pairs; Decider_NGramCPS_Hardcoded_Parameters.v likewise). So the actual working epistemology is: "which finite quotient of the tape monoid to try next" is treated as a stochastic bandit over cache policies, and the theorem statement inherits empirically tuned constants. Nobody's summary mentions this; the repo README is 8 lines. The implicit dictionary — cache-eviction policies = a parameterized basis for finite-index right congruences of word streams, empirically ordered by certificate yield — is, as far as I could find, not written down anywhere in the abstract-interpretation or automata literature (trace partitioning and history abstractions exist, but not eviction-policy-indexed congruence families, and not bandit-scheduled domain selection).
dead_ends: (1) No written notes exist for mxdys's 2026 FAR variant: the BB(6) wiki page says only "a new FAR method by mxdys was able to decide 113 of the 1534 holdouts (code on GitHub)" — the technique is documented ONLY as code, so I reverse-engineered main.cpp directly; searches for a writeup found nothing. (2) main.cpp contains a commented-out `namespace FAR{ struct NFA ... }` block (lines 2133-2182) that looks like the core decider but is dead code — the live FAR::Decider is at line 1747; cost me a detour. (3) docs.bbchallenge.org is unreachable from this network as warned; the CoqBB5_release list of per-machine methods couldn't be pulled, so per-decider counts come from the paper's Tables 3-7 only. (4) I searched for established literature on "LRU/cache-eviction abstract domains" and on any theory predicting the pipeline's occupancy cascade (175M/6M/6.6K/23/17/13) — found trace partitioning, view abstraction, and algorithm-portfolio bandits as adjacent, but no direct analogue for either; those two negatives are the basis for the 'new twist' calls above. (5) decider-bouncers-reproduction has no README; extracted the certificate semantics from src/formula_tape/mod.rs doctests and Bouncers.v instead. (6) Could not determine from public sources which exact abstraction arm killed which of the 113 BB6 holdouts (log.txt not in repo), so the claim 'congruence templates beat raw DFA enumeration' rests on the BB5-era comparison (NGramCPS 6M machines vs FAR's 23 at DFA size ≤12) rather than BB6 per-machine data.

translations.technique: Regular CTL (Closed Tape Language) — the umbrella framework
translations.what_it_actually_does: BB5 paper Sec. 4.2: a decider exhibits a set C of configurations with (1) the all-0 initial configuration in C, (2) C closed under one TM step, (3) C disjoint from halting configurations; then M never halts. All Coq-BB5 deciders except WFAR instantiate C as a REGULAR language of word-encoded configurations (tape-left, head state, tape-right), with zero-padding invariance (u∈C ⇔ 0u∈C ⇔ u0∈C). The wiki's concrete presentation: two DFAs scan the two half-tapes toward the head; the certificate is an accept set A ⊆ L×R×Q×Σ of (left-DFA state, right-DFA state, TM state, scanned symbol) tuples closed under the four one-step propagation rules. FAR is the time-reversed complement ('co-CTL': regular set containing all eventually-halting configurations, missing the initial one) — the deciders README proves the two are interconvertible by complementation/contraposition.
translations.math_translation: Theorem (CTL): Let M be a TM with configuration space Conf, step relation →, halting set H, initial c0. If ∃C ⊆ Conf with c0∈C, ∀c∈C ∀c′ (c→c′ ⇒ c′∈C), and C∩H=∅, then ¬∃n: c0 →ⁿ H. Regular CTL adds: C is a regular language over word-encodings closed under the two-sided monoid action of appending/stripping 0s. This is exactly a safety proof by inductive invariant, with the invariant drawn from a decidable-membership, decidable-inclusion class; the head-split form (L×R×Q×Σ accept set) says the invariant is recognized by a product of finite quotients of the two half-tape monoids — i.e., an invariant of finite syntactic complexity on each side.

nearest_known_math.theory: Abstract interpretation / inductive invariants (Cousot & Cousot 1977); safety verification by over-approximation of the reachable set
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — CTL is verbatim an inductive invariant argument; the community credits the idea to Marxen–Buntrock (1990) and Ligocki's 2022 blog, i.e., they know it is abstract interpretation folklore even if not by that name

nearest_known_math.theory: Regular model checking (Bouajjani–Jonsson–Nilsson–Touili 2000; Abdulla et al.): configurations as words, reachable sets over-approximated by regular languages closed under a transducer
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the framework, overlaps for practice — the TM step relation is a 3-state functional transducer, so regular CTL = finding a regular inductive invariant of a transducer system; RMC theory contributes acceleration/widening, bbchallenge contributes brutal empirical search at scale

nearest_known_math.theory: Symbolic dynamics: closed shift-invariant supersets of an orbit closure avoiding a forbidden cylinder
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the zero-padding-invariant regular C is a sofic-like cover of the orbit closure of the two-sided tape dynamics; the head-anchored (non-shift-invariant) structure is the point of departure from classical subshift theory

translations.fusion_opportunity: State the CTL family as: choose a finite-index right congruence ≈_L on left half-tapes and ≈_R on right half-tapes; the invariant is a union of (≈_L × Q×Σ × ≈_R)-classes; soundness is closure of the induced quotient system. Then all five deciders differ only in HOW the congruence is presented (n-gram windows, RLE thresholds, explicit DFA, weighted refinement), and RMC/abstract-interpretation theory supplies the missing meta-theorems: Galois connections order the deciders, and completeness results (e.g., view abstraction is complete for WQO systems) say when a tier suffices.
translations.collatz_application: The Collatz map on base-2 strings (LSD-first) is computed by a small functional transducer (×3+carry fused with shift-by-parity), so 'Collatz certificate' = regular inductive invariant of a one-transducer RMC system — precisely what our program searched and what fails through 7 DFA states in base 2. The BB5 lesson: exhaustive small-DFA search (FAR needed sizes 8-12 for the 23 hardest 5-state machines, at 30s-20min per machine per depth) is the WRONG way to find regular invariants of large index; closure-generated congruences (NGramCPS/RepWL style) reached effective DFA sizes in the thousands and killed 6,005,142 machines. So 'regular fails through size 7' rules out only low-index regular invariants; the next probe should generate congruences by closure, not enumeration.

translations.technique: Bouncers (busycoq Bouncers.v, 718 lines, + decider-bouncers-reproduction formula tapes)
translations.what_it_actually_does: Bouncers.v defines symbolic tapes: lists of segments Symbol(x) | Repeat(w), where Repeat(w) matches w^{k+1} for ANY k≥0 (relation s ~ t, match_repeat constructor). The certificate (verify_bouncer_l) is: run n0 concrete steps to reach state q at the left tape edge; split the right tape into a symbolic tape t0 via given split points; then take n1 SYMBOLIC steps, where a step is either a simple TM step on a Symbol segment or a 'shift rule' (try_shift_rule): one bounded concrete run certifying submultistep tm (S n) (q,(L,s,tail)) = (q,(L,[],tail++s′)), which lemma shiftrule_left_exec lifts BY INDUCTION ON n to ∀n: q <(s^{n+1} · l | tail · r) →+ q <(l | tail · s′^{n+1} · r) — the head chews through an arbitrary power of block s, emitting the same power of s′. After n1 symbolic steps the machine must return to state q at the edge with symbolic tape t1 such that subsumes t1 t0 (greedy decision of L(t1) ⊆ L(t0), sound only after 'alignment': rotating repeater words by conjugacy, shift_repeat: Repeat(x·r)·Symbol(x) ≡ Symbol(x)·Repeat(r·x), implemented with an explicit Cycle type for cyclic rotation). Conclusion via progress_nonhalt_cond: coinductive nonhalting. The reproduction code shows the certificate is just: formula tape + list of shift rules, e.g. '110A>(11) → (00)110A>'.
translations.math_translation: Theorem (Bouncers): Let M be a TM. Suppose ∃ state q, words u_0,…,u_k, v_1,…,v_k, and a finite set of 'crossing lemmas' each of the form (q_i, tail_i, s_i, s_i′, m_i) verified by ONE m_i-step bounded run, such that the language L = {u_0 v_1^{n_1} u_1 ⋯ v_k^{n_k} u_k : n_i ≥ 1} satisfies: (i) c0 →^{n0} (q, edge, some w ∈ L); (ii) ∀w ∈ L: (q, edge, w) →⁺ (q, edge, w′) for some w′ ∈ L — where (ii) is certified by ONE symbolic computation whose shift-rule steps carry the universal quantifier over the exponents n_i (each shift rule is proven ∀n by induction from a single concrete period-crossing). Then M does not halt. Quantifier structure: a finite certificate simultaneously proves a ∀n⃗ ∈ ℕ^k family of infinite progressions — the machine's return map on {q}×L is total and L-preserving. Dynamically: the orbit is a linearly-growing self-similar family; each bounce applies a word morphism fixing each v_i and appending a bounded number of new period blocks (hence quadratic running time), i.e., the community discovered and formally verified substitution/self-similar structure inside TM dynamics, comparing periodic tapes correctly modulo conjugacy of the period word.

nearest_known_math.theory: Acceleration / meta-transitions in infinite-state model checking (Boigelot–Wolper; regular model checking acceleration, Jonsson–Nilsson 2000, Abdulla–Jonsson–Nilsson–d'Orso 'simple and efficient' 2002; flat acceleration à la FAST/Bardin–Finkel–Leroux)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the shift rule is exactly the computed transitive closure of a loop of the transducer acting on w^n families ('meta-transition'), and subsumption-into-Repeat is a widening; but RMC acceleration theorems target length-preserving transducer relations, while Bouncers handles the head-anchored growing-tape case with a bespoke, formally verified induction (shiftrule_left_exec)

nearest_known_math.theory: Hennie's crossing-sequence method for one-tape TMs (1965)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — a shift rule says the head's crossing behavior over ONE period of a periodic tape region determines it over ALL periods (with state q recurring); this is the crossing-sequence periodicity idea turned from a lower-bound tool into a positive certificate

nearest_known_math.theory: Substitution dynamical systems / D0L word morphisms and S-adic towers
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps, and strictly weaker on the dynamics side — the bounce return-map is a constant morphism plus symbol appending, the degenerate (single, eventually-constant) case of an S-adic expansion; no bbchallenge machinery yet handles genuinely S-adic (level-2, exponentially self-similar) machines, which is exactly where Coq-BB5 needed 'sporadic' individual proofs

nearest_known_math.theory: Conjugacy of words (Lothaire, combinatorics on words): u·v vs v·u, primitive roots, Fine–Wilf
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the alignment step — Bouncers.v's Cycle/align machinery re-implements comparison of ω-periodic words modulo conjugate rotation of the period

translations.fusion_opportunity: Bouncers = acceleration where the accelerated relation is verified by induction inside a proof assistant, making 'discovered self-similarity' a first-class certificate object. Fuse with S-adic theory: replace the single formula tape by a SEQUENCE of formula tapes relating consecutive scales via a nontrivial morphism σ (config(k+1) = σ(config(k))), verified by the same shift-rule induction — this is precisely the certificate shape of Skelet #17's proof (Xu: (0,2,4,…,2^{2k},0) ↦ (0,2,4,…,2^{2k+2},0)) and of counter machines, and it is the formal home for 'level-2 inductive rules' that the BB6 wiki says current simulators handle ad hoc. RMC theory would call this 'acceleration of a non-flat loop nest'; nobody has the general verified version.
translations.collatz_application: The formula-tape + shift-rule format is exactly the lemma format of Collatz partial results: 'for all k, trajectory of pattern P(k) reaches pattern Q(f(k))'. Known predictable Collatz segments are shift rules verbatim: a binary string ending in 1^b undergoes b consecutive odd steps with a computable carry cascade — a crossing lemma over the repeater 1^b, provable by one period-crossing plus induction. Concrete program: run the Bouncers guesser (formula_tape_guessing.rs logic) on iterated Collatz-transducer traces of structured seeds (2^k−1, (4^k−1)/3, Mersenne-adjacent families) to auto-discover closed formula-tape families; any closed family avoiding the encoding of 1 would certify a divergent orbit or nontrivial cycle (so expect closure to FAIL — but the failure mode, i.e., which repeater blocks refuse to align, is diagnostic of where self-similarity breaks; per Michel 2015 that breakage IS the Collatz difficulty). For our certificate program the transferable asset is the subsumption-after-conjugacy-alignment test: certificates for parametrized configuration families config(a,b) →⁺ config(f(a,b)) with mechanically checkable induction.

translations.technique: NGramCPS (n-gram Closed Position Set) with fixed-length-history and LRU tape-alphabet augmentations
translations.what_it_actually_does: BB5 paper Algorithm 2: maintain sets L, R ⊆ A^n of 'seen' left/right n-grams and a set C of local configurations (left n-gram, TM state, scanned symbol, right n-gram). A right move pushes the left window's outermost symbol into L permanently and regenerates the unknown new rightmost symbol by globbing: for EVERY previously seen right n-gram r ∈ R whose first n−1 symbols match the current window's suffix, add the corresponding local configuration. Iterate to least fixed point; if no local configuration has an undefined (halting) transition, output NONHALT. Killed 6,005,142 of the non-loop BB5 machines (99.89% of them). Augmentations replace the tape alphabet by A × H where H records per-cell visit history: fixed-length list of the last h (state,symbol) visits (887,093 machines), or an LRU-ordered duplicate-free list of all (state,symbol) pairs ever seen at that cell, most recent first (182 machines unreachable any other way, including 'fractal-looking' machines).
translations.math_translation: Definition (implicit): For a shift-like system, the order-n Markov (SFT) cover. Theorem (NGramCPS): Let γ(L,R,C) = { (…w₂w₁)·ls·(q,m)·rs·(w₁′w₂′…) : (ls,q,m,rs) ∈ C, and every length-n window of the left extension lies in the de Bruijn walk generated by L, symmetrically for R }. Algorithm 2 computes the least (L,R,C) such that γ(L,R,C) ⊇ post(γ(L,R,C)) ∪ {c₀}; if additionally γ∩H=∅ then M never halts. I.e., the reachable set's half-tape languages are over-approximated by the largest languages with the same allowed n-blocks — the canonical shift-of-finite-type over-approximation X_n of a shift space X, with X = ∩ₙ X_n (Lind–Marcus Markov approximation), used as an inductive invariant; equivalently the invariant is a strictly-locally-testable-style language on each side. The augmentation theorem (implicit): compute the SFT cover of the LIFTED system over alphabet A×H and project; since projections of local languages are exactly the regular languages (Medvedev's theorem), history lifts recover regular invariants that are not locally testable — the community operationalized Medvedev's theorem with a cache policy, without knowing either name.

nearest_known_math.theory: Markov/SFT approximations of subshifts (Lind & Marcus, symbolic dynamics standard); SFT approximation of cellular-automaton limit sets (Culik–Pachl–Yu; Hurd)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the closure object — the n-gram closure IS the order-n SFT cover; new is only the head-anchored two-sided version with incremental (L,R,C) generation

nearest_known_math.theory: View abstraction for parameterized verification (Abdulla, Haziza, Holík, VMCAI 2013 / STTT 2016): abstract a configuration by its set of size-k sub-views, compute closure, complete for a class of WQO systems
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps strongly — same abstraction functor (bounded windows, concretization = all configurations consistent with seen views) in a different community; NGramCPS's directional L/R split and exact head window are TM-specific refinements; the WQO completeness theory on the verification side has no bbchallenge counterpart and predicts when n-gram closure must succeed

nearest_known_math.theory: Strictly locally testable languages and Medvedev's theorem (every regular language is a letter-to-letter projection of a local language); McNaughton–Papert local testability
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for expressivity — SLT_n is a strict hierarchy inside regular, and history-augmentation is exactly the Medvedev lift that closes the gap to full regular; this classifies exactly which regular-CTL machines plain NGramCPS can never solve

translations.fusion_opportunity: The LRU augmentation is abstraction refinement by per-cell visit history with an eviction policy — a refinement operator OUTSIDE the classical CEGAR loop. Fusion: view abstraction's completeness theorem (for monotonic/WQO systems, view closure at some k decides safety) + Medvedev lifts gives a principled ladder: (window size n) × (history depth h) × (eviction policy), with a theorem-shaped question nobody has posed: characterize the regular invariants reachable at level (n,h,LRU) — i.e., which syntactic monoids embed in the n-gram-of-history quotient. The 182-machine LRU residue is empirical evidence this ladder is strictly finer than the SLT_n ladder.
translations.collatz_application: Negative theorem available by fusion: Laarhoven–de Weger (arXiv:1209.3495) prove the Collatz graph mod 2^n IS the binary de Bruijn graph B(2,n), and the 2-adic extension is conjugate (Bernstein–Lagarias) to the shift, which is ergodic for Haar measure (Matthews–Watts school). Consequently the n-gram closure of essentially any seed set saturates the full de Bruijn graph: the order-n SFT cover of Collatz forward-reachable sets is the full shift for every n, so NGramCPS-type local certificates can prove NOTHING about Collatz proper — matching and explaining our observation that regular search fails at small size, and predicting that window-growth is hopeless while ANCHORED structure (MSD carry boundary, where the word ends) is the only regular resource left. Positive use: history augmentation is anchored (histories are seeded at the boundary), so the right analogue for Collatz is carry/parity-history-augmented digits, not wider windows — concretely, run n-gram closure over the alphabet {0,1}×(last h (step-parity, carry) pairs) on the Collatz transducer; this searches a corner of regular-invariant space provably disjoint from what raw ≤7-state DFA enumeration covered.
sources_read: https://arxiv.org/abs/1209.3495 (Laarhoven & de Weger, via search verification)
http://www.numbertheory.org/3x+1/ (Matthews, ergodicity of generalized Collatz maps, via search verification)

translations.technique: RepWL (Repeated Word List) + mxdys's RWL_mod refinement
translations.what_it_actually_does: BB5 paper Algorithm 3: fix block length l and threshold T. Encode the tape as a run-length sequence of length-l blocks; any block repeated ≥T times becomes w^{T+} ('T or more'). Explore the graph of such regex configurations: when the head faces a constant block, simulate concretely up to B steps ('block simulation'); when it faces w^{T+}, branch into the two cases w^{T-1}·w (exponent exactly T, per wiki: reduce to T−1 after peeling) and w·w^{T+} (still ≥T). If the graph closes within N nodes with no halting configuration: NONHALT. 6,577 BB5 machines, each with individually grid-searched (l,T) ∈ [1,38]×[2,4] hardcoded in the Coq proof; RepWL graphs ranged 42-143,181 nodes. mxdys's RWL_mod (main.cpp:1154) refines the exponent abstraction: each run-length is tracked as the pair (min(count,T), count mod m) — threshold AND congruence.
translations.math_translation: Each graph node denotes a bounded regular language u₁^{e₁}⋯u_k^{e_k} with e_i ∈ {exact value < T} ∪ {≥T}; the branching rule is the ideal decomposition w^{≥T} = w^T ∪ w·w^{≥T}. Theorem (RepWL): if the finite graph G of such nodes satisfies post(⟦node⟧) ⊆ ⋃⟦successors⟧ for every node (certified by block simulation + branching) and no node's language meets H, then M never halts (CTL with C = union of node languages). The exponent abstraction ℕ → {0,…,T−1, ≥T} is the classical counter abstraction; RWL_mod's (threshold, modulus) pairs are exactly the general finite quotients of the monogenic semigroup (ℕ,+1) — every finite abstraction of unary counting has this form (eventual periodicity of finite monogenic semigroups) — so RWL_mod-representable invariants are precisely the regular languages that factor through run-length encoding, and the community has (unknowingly) implemented the UNIVERSAL finite abstraction of run-length exponents.

nearest_known_math.theory: (0,1,…,T−1,∞) counter abstraction in parameterized verification (Pnueli–Xu–Zuck 2002 and folklore)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the exponent domain — identical abstraction, different carrier (tape run-lengths instead of process counters)

nearest_known_math.theory: Simple regular expressions as ideal representations for lossy channel systems (Abdulla–Bouajjani–Jonsson 1998): downward-closed word languages as finite unions of products of atoms with * exponents
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — RepWL nodes are the upward-closed-in-exponent analogue (ideals of (ℕ^k,≤) glued onto block products); the WQO/ideal theory (finite ideal decompositions, completeness of ideal-based reachability à la Finkel–Goubault-Larrecq) exists on the verification side and has no bbchallenge counterpart

nearest_known_math.theory: Parikh's theorem / semilinear sets, Ginsburg–Spanier bounded languages
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps weakly — RepWL's languages are bounded regular; genuinely semilinear constraints BETWEEN exponents (n₁=n₂) are exactly what RepWL cannot express and what the Finned machines needed (linear relation among 3 unary counters — solved only by WFAR-style handcrafted certificates)

translations.fusion_opportunity: RepWL + WQO ideal theory: because the exponent order (ℕ^k,≤) is a WQO, RepWL closure is guaranteed to terminate on any machine whose reach-set RLE profile is downward/upward-closed-generated — a completeness theorem the community lacks (they instead bound by N nodes and grid-search (l,T)). Conversely RWL_mod's modulus coordinate leaves WQO-land (congruences aren't monotone) — the correct fused domain is 'ideal × congruence', i.e., Presburger-definable exponent sets with threshold+mod atoms, which is exactly the expressive closure of run-length regular invariants. A clean theorem to write: RepWL_{l,T,m} certificates over all (T,m) capture exactly the regular CTL invariants recognizable by automata reading RLE(l)-blocks; strictness in (T,m) follows from the finite-monogenic-semigroup classification.
translations.collatz_application: Run-length encoding is THE natural Collatz coordinate: the RLE of n's binary expansion transforms under the Collatz map by a Collatz-like map on the exponent tuple (trailing 1-run of length b ⇒ b consecutive odd steps; the Terras parity vector is a function of the RLE), which is why Collatz heuristics (Kontorovich–Lagarias stochastic models) live on run-lengths. Concrete transfer: apply RepWL with l=1 (raw 0/1 runs) and RWL_mod's (threshold, mod-3) exponent abstraction to the Collatz transducer — mod-3 counting of run-lengths is the automata shadow of the 3-adic bookkeeping that any Collatz argument needs. Prediction from the hierarchy data: this tier killed the 'counter' zoology at BB5 (base-2/3/Fibonacci counters), and Collatz-as-TM is precisely a coupled double counter (×3 vs ÷2), so RepWL-with-mod is the lowest tier with any a-priori chance on Collatz sub-statements (e.g., certifying no divergent orbit stays within a fixed RLE shape family) — while plain RepWL (threshold only) provably saturates for the same ergodicity reason as NGramCPS.

translations.technique: FAR (Finite Automata Reduction) — co-CTL by NFA with Boolean-matrix simulation conditions
translations.what_it_actually_does: Certificate = an NFA (q₀, {T_γ}, a) over the Boolean semiring plus a 'steady state-set' row vector s, satisfying paper Theorem 4.9: q₀T₀=q₀ and T₀aᵀ=aᵀ (zero-padding invariance); saᵀ=1, sT₀⪰s, sT₁⪰s (steady set is accepting and self-sustaining); ∀ halting rules (f,r): ∀u: q₀T_uT_fT_r ⪰ s; for left rules (f,r)→(w,L,t): T_tT_bT_w ⪯ T_bT_fT_r for both b; for right rules: T_fT_r ⪰ T_wT_t; and q₀T_Aaᵀ=0 (initial configuration rejected). Then the NFA's language contains all eventually-halting configurations but not the initial one ⇒ nonhalt. In Coq-BB5 FAR exists only as a verifier for 23 hardcoded certificates (+17 WFAR) found by weeks of brute-force DFA search or translated from 'other, undocumented, regular co-CTL methods'. The deciders-repo README derives the whole method from a quotient construction: pick a finite-index left congruence (a DFA on the left half-tape); the quotiented TM is a pushdown system; cite Bouajjani–Esparza–Maler 1997 to compute the regular set of halt-reaching configurations by saturation; prove that refining any co-CTL L by its left syntactic congruence yields L′ ⊆ L — hence FAR is UNIVERSAL for regular co-CTL (any regular certificate can be shoehorned into Theorem 4.9's normal form).
translations.math_translation: Theorem (FAR universality, implicit in README): For every TM M and every regular co-CTL L (zero-padding invariant, backward-closed under steps, containing H-configurations, avoiding c₀), the left-syntactic-congruence quotient of M is a pushdown system P, and pre*(halting configs of P) is an effectively regular co-CTL L′ ⊆ L presentable in the Theorem 4.9 matrix normal form with DFA = the congruence automaton. So: ∃ regular certificate ⟺ ∃ finite-index left congruence whose induced pushdown saturation avoids c₀ — the search space collapses from 'all regular languages' to 'all finite quotients of the left tape monoid'. The matrix inequalities themselves state that u ↦ q₀T_u is a monoid morphism into Boolean matrices under which the TM's rewriting relation is monotone (a simulation quasi-order), i.e., a recognizable compatible quasi-model of the string-rewriting system.

nearest_known_math.theory: Pushdown-system saturation: pre*/post* of a regular set is regular, computed by automaton saturation (Büchi 1964; Bouajjani–Esparza–Maler CONCUR 1997)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by AND KNOWINGLY — the deciders README cites BEM97 explicitly and derives the recognizer; mxdys's FAR::Decider (main.cpp:1747, the H2/H3/H2b return-relation maps ret2/ret3/pre23/pre32/pre33) is a hand-rolled pre* saturation computing 'head exits block to the left' return relations

nearest_known_math.theory: Automata completion / regular over-approximation for rewriting reachability (Genet's tree-automata completion; Geser–Hofbauer–Waldmann match-bounds, 'Match-Bounded String Rewriting Systems' AAECC 2004 — 'sets of immortal strings are effectively regular' for match-bounded systems; Endrullis/Waldmann automata that certify termination)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — same certificate object (a finite automaton compatible with a string-rewriting relation) built by the termination community for the mirror-image problem; the match-bound literature even has the completeness/decidability theorems (which rewriting systems admit such automata) that bbchallenge lacks

nearest_known_math.theory: Matrix interpretations over semirings for rewriting (Endrullis–Waldmann–Zantema); simulation preorders in automata theory
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the verification conditions — Theorem 4.9's ⪯-inequalities are matrix interpretations over the Boolean semiring, with 'accepted steady state-set' playing the role of a top element

translations.fusion_opportunity: The twist worth extracting: FAR certificates are stored as the DFA ALONE (the NFA half is reconstructed 'almost instantly' by saturation — the DVF format is direction byte + DFA table); i.e., the left congruence is the entire information content of a regular nonhalting proof. Fused with match-bound theory this gives a research program: classify TMs by the minimal index of a certifying left congruence (a 'regular proof complexity' measure); match-bound theory proves this index is computable for match-bounded rewriting — is 5-state TM rewriting ever match-bounded, and is the 23-machine FAR residue exactly the non-match-bounded regular machines? Nobody has run Matchbox/Torpa-style certifiers on the BB holdout sets.
translations.collatz_application: Our 'regular fails through 7 states in base 2' is exactly a FAR-style search bounded at congruence index 7. Two direct transfers: (1) the MITM-DFA trick — search pairs of SMALL left/right DFAs meeting at the head instead of one big automaton; BB5 data shows mitm_dfa overtakes direct search from depth 8 (4s vs 2min per machine at depth 10-11), so our 7-state ceiling can realistically be pushed to index ~11-12 with their exact prover; (2) the universality theorem redirects effort: instead of enumerating DFAs, enumerate CONGRUENCE GENERATORS (which residues of the word matter: digit-sum mod 3^k, position of last carry, run-length parities) — for Collatz the natural candidates are the base-2 syntactic congruences of the sets {n : n ≡ r mod 3^k}, which are known to be regular with index 3^k·O(1) (multiplication automata), far beyond blind search but cheap to construct explicitly. If a regular Collatz certificate exists at all, FAR-universality says one exists whose DFA is the syntactic automaton of the certificate language itself — so failure of principled congruence families (mod-3^k towers, carry automata) at moderate index is much stronger evidence of genuine non-regularity than blind ≤7 enumeration.

translations.technique: WFAR (Weighted FAR / MITMWFAR) — ℤ-weighted two-sided automata certificates
translations.what_it_actually_does: Certificate (paper Sec 4.7, Theorem 4.10) = a deterministic weighted automaton on each half-tape (left WA reads left-to-right up to the head; right WA reads right-to-left), weights in ℤ summed along both runs into one global W = W_l + W_r, plus an accept set 𝔄 of weighted configurations [p] q,s [q'] with per-triple weight windows W≥m, W≤M (bounds in ℤ∪{±∞}). Verification: zero-symbol stability with weight 0; initial config accepted at W=0; 'weighted forward closure' — each accept element's one-step successors must be in 𝔄 after updating windows by the weight change w_L(p,b)−w_R(q',r'), EXCEPT successors whose weight window is infeasible given per-state reachable-weight intervals (computable by Bellman–Ford on the WAs; negative cycles give −∞ bounds); no halting configuration accepted. 17 BB5 machines, all suspected irregular (an irregularity proof exists for Finned #3); certificates found by brute-force WFA search; the handcrafted 'Helices' certificates needed ~50 states per side.
translations.math_translation: The certified invariant is C = { c : (l-state(c), TM state/symbol, r-state(c)) ∈ 𝔄 ∧ W(c) ∈ I_{triple} } — the language of a deterministic automaton equipped with one ℤ-valued blind counter and an interval acceptance condition. This strictly exceeds regular: it recognizes {0ⁿ1ⁿ} (paper Figure 14). Theorem (implicit): CTL with invariants recognized by (finite monoid) × (one ℤ-counter with interval constraint) is a sound nonhalting certificate class strictly above regular CTL; the infeasibility pruning says: restrict the product automaton to its reachable subsemigroup in Q × ℤ, computed exactly by shortest-path closure. The Finned machines' content: three unary counters with an invariant LINEAR relation — one ℤ-functional suffices to pin the relation, which is why weights crack them and regular cannot (pumping breaks the linear relation).

nearest_known_math.theory: Blind one-counter automata / ℤ-automata (Greibach 1978, 'blind and partially blind multicounter machines'); one-dimensional ℤ-VASS
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the acceptor class — deterministic WA with interval acceptance IS a deterministic blind ℤ-counter automaton; Greibach's strictness hierarchy (blind-k ⊊ blind-(k+1)) predicts the next certificate tiers (multi-weight WFAR), which bbchallenge has not built

nearest_known_math.theory: Parikh automata (Klaedtke–Rueß 2003) and affine Parikh automata (Cadilhac–Finkel–McKenzie): NFA + semilinear constraint on transition counts
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — WFAR = deterministic Parikh automaton with a 1-dim interval (not general semilinear) constraint; the Parikh-automata literature has the closure/decidability theory (emptiness decidable, inclusion undecidable in general) that delimits how far weighted certificate VERIFICATION can be pushed before it becomes as hard as the problem

nearest_known_math.theory: Linear/affine invariants in program analysis (Karr's algorithm; octagons); Bellman–Ford feasible-interval computation = single-source shortest paths as abstract fixpoint
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the pruning step — 'discard weight-infeasible configurations' is interval abstract interpretation on the weighted product graph

translations.fusion_opportunity: The genuinely nice structure is the SPLIT of one global counter across two one-way automata meeting at the head (meet-in-the-middle weighted certificate) — a communication-free decomposition of a Parikh condition. Fusing with Greibach/Parikh theory: allow k weights (semilinear windows) and you get certificates for any machine whose tape invariant is 'regular skeleton + semilinear Parikh constraint'; the BB5 sporadic list says exactly which dynamical behaviors then remain: Fibonacci counting (nonsemilinear in base-2 Parikh image — needs Zeckendorf numeration, cf. Frougny's automatic addition in Fibonacci base) and Gray-code list rewriting (Skelet #17). I.e., the next rung after WFAR is provably 'automatic sequences over exotic numeration systems', not more counters — the BB5 data empirically located the regular→Parikh→numeration-automatic boundary.
translations.collatz_application: For Collatz the canonical ℤ-weight exists: W = a·(#odd steps) − b·(#halvings) with a/b ≈ log₂3, the drift functional of Terras/Lagarias density arguments and of Eliahou-style cycle bounds (which are exactly continued-fraction rational approximations to log₂3 — i.e., choices of integer weights a,b). Transfer: build WFAR-style certificates for the Collatz transducer where the left/right WAs track digit-weighted sums (e.g., W = Σ c_i over digit patterns) and accept-windows encode 'net drift bounded'; infeasible-weight pruning is precisely the automata version of 'a divergent trajectory must have odd-step density > log2/log3'. Prediction from the hierarchy: since Finned-machine-style LINEAR relations among counters are the signature WFAR win, the Collatz sub-statements most likely certifiable at this tier are cycle-exclusions in fixed RLE shape families (where cycle existence forces an exact linear relation between run-length sums — the same relation continued-fraction arguments kill). Full Collatz sits above this tier: Antihydra and the other Cryptids (Appendix C) are Collatz-like iterations that the ENTIRE FAR+WFAR arsenal fails on, which is the strongest empirical calibration our certificate program has.

translations.technique: mxdys 2026 'new FAR variant' (ccz181078/TM branch FAR): abstraction-functor-generated DFAs + MITM-CTL/FAR saturation + UCB portfolio (killed 113/1534 BB6 holdouts on first application)
translations.what_it_actually_does: main.cpp implements two decider templates parameterized by a C++ type A: MitM_CTL::Decider<A> and FAR::Decider<A>. A is a 'DFA generator': a finite-data fold state with push(Word) consuming the half-tape block stream head-outward; the decider lazily materializes the reachable quotient automaton (DFA::push at line 1562: state = A-value, transition = apply fold, deduplicate via id map) and runs either (a) CTL closure over tuples (left-DFA-state, right-DFA-state, TM state, direction) with block-level simulation (WordUpdateLemma from bounded concrete runs), re-propagating when a lazily-added DFA edge invalidates prior closure (the upds queues), then re-checks the final set with an independent CTL_Fixedlen_verify::Verifier; or (b) pre*-saturation building return relations H2=(s,r) 's> [r]', H3=(w,s,r), H2b='<s [r]' with maps ret2/ret3/pre23/pre32/pre33 — a pushdown-system saturation over the quotient. The A-library: CPS_LRU (bounded list with LRU eviction, parameters LRUH/H/tH), CPS_LRU_BD, LRUpair/set_pair (LRU or set of adjacent block PAIRS — bigram-of-blocks memory), NGset/NG1/NG (n-gram windows), RWL_mod/RWS_mod/RNGS_mod (run-length with threshold+modulus exponents), ExRWL (on-line RLE with block doubling), unique_wall, RndDFA (randomly generated lazy DFA, seeded). ucb_gen.py enumerates ~600 (template, hyperparameter) arms; struct UCB schedules them as a multi-armed bandit with reward = solves/time, persisted in ucb.txt, exploration constant randomly toggled 1.0/0.01.
translations.math_translation: Definition (implicit): a 'DFA generator' is a computable monoid-action fold φ: S × Σ_blocks → S with finite reachable image; it induces the finite-index right congruence u ≈_φ v ⟺ φ*(s₀,u) = φ*(s₀,v), and the certificate class CTL(φ) = regular CTL invariants that are unions of ≈_φ-classes on each side. Theorem (implicit soundness): for any φ_L, φ_R, closure of the quotient system (Conf/≈_{φ_L}×≈_{φ_R}) with no reachable halting class certifies nonhalting; and (implicit search claim) the map φ ↦ certificate-yield is empirically learnable: treat {φ_i(θ)} as bandit arms and allocate compute by UCB. The mathematical shift vs classical FAR: replace enumeration over all DFAs of size ≤k (doubly exponential) by enumeration over a small library of STRUCTURED congruence families whose index can be in the thousands — i.e., search in the lattice of finite-index congruences via generators-with-hyperparameters rather than by brute force at the bottom of the lattice.

nearest_known_math.theory: Myhill–Nerode / lattice of finite-index right congruences; quotient abstractions in abstract regular model checking (Bouajjani–Habermehl–Vojnar 2004: automata quotiented by finite-height abstractions, with refinement)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the objects, overlaps for the method — ARMC quotients the transition automata by predicate-based equivalences with CEGAR refinement; mxdys instead fixes a LIBRARY of congruence templates and searches hyperparameters stochastically, with no counterexample-guided refinement at all

nearest_known_math.theory: Algorithm portfolios with bandit-based selection (SATzilla lineage; bandit algorithm selection literature)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the scheduler — UCB over solver configurations is standard; new carrier: the 'solvers' are abstract domains (certificate classes), so the bandit is empirically ordering MATHEMATICAL EXPRESSIVITY by yield-per-CPU-second

nearest_known_math.theory: Pushdown saturation (BEM97) — again, for the FAR::Decider<A> back-end
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by — the H2/H3/H2b rule schema is pre* saturation with block-compressed stack alphabet

translations.fusion_opportunity: Two fusions suggest themselves. (1) Replace the fixed template library by L*-style automata learning: the fold-generated DFAs are exactly what Angluin-style learners produce from membership/equivalence queries, and the CTL closure failure set is a ready-made counterexample oracle — this would turn the bandit-over-templates into principled congruence CEGAR (ICE learning for regular invariants exists in software verification; nobody has aimed it at Turing-machine tape invariants or Busy Beaver holdouts). (2) The bandit's empirical arm-ordering is data about which congruence families are information-efficient for 'natural' machines — fused with the Medvedev-lift view (entry 3), it estimates the distribution of minimal certifying congruence indices across the machine population, a quantity no theory currently predicts.
translations.collatz_application: Directly runnable on our problem: encode the Collatz step as the 2-symbol TM/transducer, generate the arm grid of ucb_gen.py (CPS_LRU × {LRUH∈{1..16}, H,tH∈{0..2}}, RWL_mod × {H∈{1..16}, mod∈{1..6}}, block size n∈{1..24}) and let UCB spend a CPU-month. This searches regular invariants of index orders of magnitude beyond our 7-state ceiling, biased toward the congruence families that empirically certify counter-like machines — and Collatz-as-TM is a coupled counter. The mod∈{1..6} coordinate is exactly where 3-adic structure can enter a regular certificate (digit-stream congruences mod 3). Honest calibration: the same data says the residue of this method at BB6 (1421/1534 holdouts untouched) is dominated by Collatz-like Cryptids, so expect the bandit to certify Collatz SUB-statements (shape-restricted families, cycle exclusions) at best, and treat its systematic failure pattern as evidence for genuine trans-regular invariant complexity of Collatz proper.

translations.technique: Loop deciders (Cyclers / Translated Cyclers) via transcript-only detection — the base of the hierarchy
translations.what_it_actually_does: BB5 paper Algorithm 1 + Theorem 4.4: record only the machine's TRANSCRIPT — the sequence of (state, read-symbol, head-position) along the head's worldline, never full tapes. Detect a back-to-back repetition of a length-l transcript window; then (1) if the head position at the two window-starts is equal ⇒ Cycler; (2) if the position is a running maximum (resp. minimum) at both starts and offset right (resp. left) ⇒ Translated Cycler. Correctness (Lemma 4.1, called 'surprisingly nontrivial') reconstructs equality of the relevant tape cells purely from transcript repetition + extremality. Decided 175,373,810 of 181M machines (95.5% of nonhalters, 99.99% of halters at step-limit 130/4100/1.05M).
translations.math_translation: Theorem: For a TM space-time function F_M(t, g(t)) = (tape symbol at head, state), if ∃t₀, l>0 with F_M(p_i) = F_M(p'_i) for the head-worldline points p_i (times t₀..t₀+l−1) and p'_i (times t₀+l..t₀+2l−1), and the head positions at t₀, t₀+l are both equal (case 1) or both one-sided-extremal with matching side (cases 2-3), then M's orbit is eventually periodic (modulo spatial translation) and never halts. The mathematical content: the head itinerary (symbolic coding along the moving singularity) plus extremality marks is a COMPLETE invariant for eventual periodicity — the tape outside the visited cone is known (zeros), and inside, every cell's content at its last visit is a function of the transcript. This is a discrete domain-of-dependence/characteristics statement: the space-time diagram is determined by its restriction to the head worldline.

nearest_known_math.theory: Lin's recurrence (Shen Lin's 1963 thesis, translated cyclers) and classical cycle detection (Floyd/Brent)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the objects detected are Lin's; the transcript-only algorithm is new (paper: 'completely different algorithm... introduced with Coq-BB5'), trading tape storage for itinerary storage

nearest_known_math.theory: Symbolic dynamics codings: itinerary determines orbit for expansive systems; crossing-sequence reconstructions (Hennie 1965)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by in spirit — 'transcript determines tape at revisits' is a crossing-sequence reconstruction; the extremality side-condition making it sound for translated cycles appears to be new bookkeeping

translations.fusion_opportunity: Transcript-repetition with extremality is a finite-window test for eventual periodicity-mod-translation of a one-dimensional moving-interface system. Fusion with the Morse–Hedlund theorem family: for the head itinerary sequence, subword-complexity thresholds could give PROVABLE step-limits L(machine size) beyond which non-detection implies aperiodicity of specific types — turning the empirical step-limit schedule (130 / 4100 / 1,050,000) into theorems about which loops exist at which scales (Skelet #1's pre-period 5.42×10^51 with period 8.47×10^9 shows the general bound is hopeless, but complexity-restricted versions may not be).
translations.collatz_application: For Collatz orbits, the analogue of the transcript is the parity vector (Terras): the itinerary of the orbit under the 2-adic shift-coding, which likewise determines the orbit (Bernstein–Lagarias conjugacy = exactly 'transcript determines tape'). Loop-decider logic transfers as: a repeated parity-vector window with matching '2-adic extremality' (valuation records) would certify an eventual cycle. Eliahou-type results say nontrivial cycles need period > 10^10 — the Skelet #1 phenomenon (astronomically delayed periodicity, found only by accelerated simulation) is the BB community's concrete demonstration that 'no small transcript repetition' is compatible with eventual periodicity, i.e., a warning against reading our simulation bounds as evidence about Collatz cycles.

translations.technique: The empirical certificate-expressivity hierarchy itself (pipeline occupancy: 175,373,810 loops → 6,005,142 NGramCPS → 6,577 RepWL → 23 FAR → 17 WFAR → 13 sporadic, +183 long halters, +24 1RB-reduction)
translations.what_it_actually_does: The S(5) pipeline (Table 3) stratifies all 181,385,789 enumerated 5-state machines by the WEAKEST certificate class that decides them (pipeline order confounds this slightly — each tier only sees the previous tier's residue — but the tiers are also roughly ordered by expressivity). Each successive tier catches ~99.9% of the remaining machines: residue fractions ≈ 3.4% → 0.11% of residue survives NGramCPS → 0.35% of that survives RepWL → then 40, 17, 13. The sporadic 13 decompose into: 5 Finned (three unary counters with a linear invariant), 5 Shift Overflow Counters (two binary counters + reset invariant), Skelet #1 (translated cycler, pre-period 5.42×10^51), Skelet #10 (double Fibonacci counter — Zeckendorf numeration), Skelet #17 (Gray-code list rewriting, Xu's parametrized-family induction). At BB6 the frontier is Cryptids (Antihydra etc.) — explicitly Collatz-like iterations.
translations.math_translation: The instantiated mathematical hierarchy of invariant classes, per side of the tape: finite/eventually-periodic orbits ⊂ order-n SFT covers (≈ strictly locally testable languages; strict in n) ⊂ RLE-threshold ideals (SRE-like bounded languages; with mod-counting = all RLE-factorable regular) ⊂ regular (FAR tier — provably UNIVERSAL for regular certificates, so within-regular pipeline distinctions are search-efficiency, not expressivity) ⊂ regular × one ℤ-counter with interval windows (deterministic Parikh/blind-counter; strictly above regular by 0ⁿ1ⁿ) ⊂ numeration-automatic and ad hoc arithmetic invariants (Zeckendorf-base counting à la Frougny; Gray-code dynamics; parametrized-family induction). Known strictness theorems exist at every boundary (SLT_n strict, McNaughton–Papert local testability ⊊ regular; regular ⊊ blind-1-counter, Greibach; blind-k ⊊ blind-(k+1)). The forcing meta-theorem: nonhalting-from-c₀ is Π⁰₁-complete while each certificate class with decidable verification certifies a Σ⁰₁ set — so EVERY sound tier leaves a nonempty residue and the tower provably never closes (Gödel–Turing diagonal). What NO known theorem predicts: the occupancy profile — why each tier's residue shrinks by ~10³ on the 5-state population, i.e., the distribution of minimal-invariant-complexity over random small programs. This is genuinely uncharted: an empirical 'proof-complexity zoology' with no analogue in the invariant-generation literature (termination competitions observe similar cascades but nobody has theorized them).

nearest_known_math.theory: Subregular and counter hierarchies: SLT_n / locally testable ⊊ star-free ⊊ regular (McNaughton–Papert, Brzozowski–Simon); Greibach's blind-counter hierarchy; Parikh automata; SFT ⊊ sofic ⊊ effective subshifts (Weiss; symbolic dynamics)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps — the pipeline tiers instantiate these classes on half-tape languages almost exactly (NGramCPS=SLT/SFT, augmented=Medvedev-lifted regular, RepWL=bounded/ideal, FAR=regular, WFAR=blind-1-counter), so all known strictness theorems apply; but the hierarchies' theory says nothing about occupancy counts

nearest_known_math.theory: Π⁰₁-completeness of totality/nonhalting + c.e.-ness of certified sets (Gödel incompleteness in certificate form; Aaronson's Busy Beaver frontier survey)
nearest_known_math.relationship: subsumed-by for the non-collapse — 'no finite tower suffices' is classical; the BB5/BB6 data adds the first MEASUREMENT of where small machines actually fall

nearest_known_math.theory: Numeration systems and automatic sequences (Frougny: addition in Fibonacci/Zeckendorf base is finite-automaton computable; Akiyama–Frougny–Sakarovitch rational-base numeration)
nearest_known_math.relationship: overlaps at the frontier tier — Skelet #10's 'double Fibonacci counter' is automatic in Zeckendorf base though wildly non-regular in base-2 tape coordinates: the top of the BB hierarchy is 'regular after a change of numeration', which is a precise, known-math description of what 'sporadic' means

translations.fusion_opportunity: Conjecture worth formalizing (the hierarchy's shape as a theorem-target): define for each nonhalting TM its certificate rank = least tier in the (SLT_n, RLE-ideal, regular, +ℤ-counter, numeration-automatic) tower admitting an invariant; the BB5 census is the first computed rank distribution and it is heavy-tailed like a description-length prior (each rank ~10⁻³ of previous). Fusing with algorithmic information theory: rank should be lower-bounded by the invariant's Kolmogorov complexity relative to the machine, giving a Levin-style prediction of the cascade shape — testable against the BB6 census as it completes. This would be a new kind of theorem: average-case proof complexity over the space of small programs.
translations.collatz_application: Calibrated placement of Collatz: (a) BB champions provably simulate Collatz-like functions (Michel 1993/2015), (b) the BB6 frontier objects (Antihydra, Hydra, other Cryptids) ARE Collatz-like iterations on which every tier through WFAR + the 2026 congruence-portfolio fails, (c) Matthews–Watts ergodicity + the de Bruijn identification make the two bottom tiers provably useless for Collatz proper, and (d) our own data point — regular fails through congruence index 7 in base 2 — is consistent with rank ≥ regular-with-large-index or above. Prediction from the hierarchy's shape and the Skelet #10 lesson: if Collatz has a certificate in any automata-theoretic tier, it will be 'regular after a change of numeration' — the actionable move is to re-coordinate: rational-base 3/2 numeration (Akiyama–Frougny–Sakarovitch — already in our papers directory) or Zeckendorf-style codings in which BOTH Collatz branches become letter-local transducers, then re-run the FAR/portfolio machinery in the new coordinates. If regular-after-renumeration also fails systematically, the BB analogy says Collatz sits with the Cryptids: needing parametrized-family induction (Bouncers-at-level-2 / Xu-style) — i.e., certificates that are theorems-with-quantifiers rather than automata. Either outcome makes our certificate program's negative results interpretable: they measure Collatz's rank in a hierarchy whose lower tiers are now completely mapped.
